metadata.title: Ship It: Three Canvas Submissions
metadata.playlist: Build the Recipe — Make Your Tool Conductor-Ready
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 5B brief — Nik's Recipe-Based Framework

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 6 of 6. Three separate files, specific names, and two twenty-point deductions if you bundle them wrong. The PDF has everything in order. The config and the recipe are separate files. The deduction list is the fastest final check — run through it on camera and you will not lose forty points because you submitted the config inside the PDF instead of as a standalone JSON. Let's close the assignment.

props.command: 3 separate files: PDF + brand_config.json + recipe.md. Run deduction checklist before submitting.
props.runningText: config and recipe must be separate files — -20 each if bundled only in the PDF…
output: PDF: LastName_FirstName_A5B_BuildTheRecipe.pdf — all four parts + appendices

beats.act: the pdf — four parts in order
beats.narration_text: The PDF is named LastName_FirstName_A5B_BuildTheRecipe.pdf. It contains four parts in order, each as a labeled section. Part 1: the brand_config documentation — a written explanation of each config field and the brief section it came from. Not the JSON itself — the documentation of the JSON. The JSON is the separate file. Part 2: the verified data folder section — the README text from data/verified/, the record counts per source, the quality gate applied, and the rejection summary. Part 3: the recipe — the full plain-language recipe from Episode 4 in the PDF. Part 4: the /snickerdoodle documentation — the description you used, every TODO the audit generated with its type, the resolution for each closed TODO, and the real blocker for each open one. Appendices: the verified data README as a standalone appendix, and any supporting materials the grader needs to assess the submission. The order matters. A PDF where the grader has to hunt for the config documentation or the /snickerdoodle run is a PDF that is harder to grade.

artifactLines: Part 1: brand_config documentation — explain each field + which brief section it maps to.
Part 2: verified data folder — README text, counts, gate applied, rejection summary.
Part 3: the recipe — full plain-language recipe from E4.
Part 4: /snickerdoodle run — description used · every TODO (type + text) · resolutions · open blockers.
Appendices: verified data README + any supporting materials.
props.sparkLine: Four parts in order. Config documentation ≠ the JSON itself.

beats.narration_text: In addition to the PDF, Canvas receives two separate files. The brand_config.json — the validated JSON file, submitted as a standalone file, not embedded in the PDF. And the recipe — the plain-language recipe file as a standalone Markdown or text file, named recipe_[YourLastName].md or recipe_[YourLastName].txt. Neither of these is optional. Submitting the config only inside the PDF — not as a separate file — costs twenty points. Submitting the recipe only inside the PDF costs another twenty. That is forty points for a file organization error. Now the deduction checklist. Run through it before you hit Submit. Minus twenty: config not submitted as a separate file. Minus twenty: recipe not submitted as a separate file. Minus ten per gate: phase gates missing judgment types — open the recipe, read each gate, confirm every one has a PA, PF, TO, IJ, or EI. Minus fifteen: any of the four PDF sections missing. And minus ten: open TODOs in the /snickerdoodle documentation with no real blocker. That is the full deduction list. Each one is a checkable item. Check them.

artifactLines: -10/gate: phase gate missing judgment type — open recipe, read each gate.
-10: open TODOs with no real blocker in /snickerdoodle section.
props.sparkLine: Config = separate file. Recipe = separate file. -20 each if not.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Ship It. Three Canvas submissions: the PDF named LastName_FirstName_A5B_BuildTheRecipe.pdf with four sections in order — config documentation, verified folder, recipe, /snickerdoodle run — plus appendices; the brand_config.json as a standalone file; and the recipe as recipe_[LastName].md or .txt as a standalone file. Deduction checklist before submitting: config as separate file, recipe as separate file, every phase gate has a judgment type, all four PDF sections present, no open TODOs without real blockers. The config and recipe are portfolio pieces — they are proof that you know how to define failure modes before running AI, which most people who use AI never do. That is the through-line from Episode 1 to here. Ship it.

artifactLines: PDF: 4 sections (config doc · verified folder · recipe · snickerdoodle) + appendices.
Checklist: -20 config not separate · -20 recipe not separate · -10/gate no type · -15 section missing · -10 open TODO no blocker.
Config + recipe = portfolio proof: you defined failure modes before running AI.
Most people who use AI never do that. Ship it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Two things before you submit. First: run your brand_config.json through a JSON validator one final time. Open jsonlint.com, paste the entire file, click Validate. If it passes, the file is ready. If it fails, read the error message, fix the issue, and validate again. Do not submit a file that fails validation — invalid JSON is ungradeable for the config section. Second: read your recipe out loud to someone who has never seen your project — a roommate, a classmate, a family member. Tell them nothing about the project first. Read from the executive summary to the conductor note. When they ask a question or look confused, that sentence is on your revision list. The parts of the recipe that are clear to you because you built the pipeline are not necessarily clear to a first-time conductor. The read-aloud test catches the gaps that reading silently to yourself does not. Fix the gaps. Validate the JSON. Submit three files. Done.

props.command: Run brand_config.json through a JSON validator (jsonlint.com). Then read the recipe aloud to someone who has never seen the project — when they ask a question or look confused, that sentence is your revision list. Fix the gaps. Validate the JSON. Submit 3 files.
props.runningText: the read-aloud test catches gaps that reading silently doesn't — run it before submitting…

beats.narration_text: Ship It: Three Canvas Submissions. Liam, in for Bear.
